postData.contentHtml: The 2025 Detroit Lions season was marred by what can only be described as the 'complete and utter destruction' of their secondary, ranking as the second most impactful injury news for the team that year. By an astounding Week 7, every single member of the starting secondary was on the injured list, forcing the Lions to deploy an unheralded group of backups affectionately dubbed the 'Legion of Whom.' Miraculously, this makeshift unit rose to the occasion, delivering an impressive performance in a 24-9 victory over the Tampa Bay Buccaneers. The widespread and severe nature of these injuries has significant ramifications extending into the 2026 season. Key players whose availability remains highly uncertain include: * Kerby Joseph (S): Missed 11 games in 2025 due to a left knee injury, facing a daunting 10/10 medical concern level for 2026, making his return for a substantial portion of the season highly questionable. * Brian Branch (S): Sidelined for 4 games with a right Achilles injury, he carries an 8/10 medical concern level into 2026, with his availability also uncertain for a significant period. * Terrion Arnold (CB): After missing 9 games due to two shoulder surgeries and a concussion, Arnold was subsequently released from the team following eight felony charges, creating a major void in the secondary. * Ennis Rakestraw (CB): Missed the entirety of the 2025 season (17 games) after shoulder surgery, entering 2026 with a 6/10 medical concern level. Other notable impact players included DJ Reed (hamstring), Dan Jackson (leg), Thomas Harper (concussions), Khalil Dorsey (concussion, wrist), and Avonte Maddox (hamstring, illness, back), all contributing to the staggering list of sidelined talent. The 'next man up' philosophy was tested to its absolute limits, with the sheer volume of injuries nearly exhausting the team's depth. In response to this crisis, the Lions actively sought to bolster their secondary depth during the offseason, bringing in several new faces with low medical concern levels. These additions include Keith Abney (CB), Aamaris Brown (CB), De’Shawn Rucker (CB), Chuck Clark (S), Roger McCreary (CB), and Christian Izien (CB/S). While these moves are crucial for rebuilding, the lingering reliability questions surrounding several returning players mean that the 'Legion of Whom' may not be a temporary solution but rather a permanent fixture in the 2026 defensive lineup. Interestingly, social media sentiment surrounding the Detroit Lions' secondary injuries shows a mix of concern and optimism, with discussions being 'mostly positive, some debate.' This suggests fans are aware of the challenges but perhaps also acknowledge the team's efforts to adapt and remain competitive despite adversity.
postData.title: Detroit Lions' Secondary Decimated in 2025: A Look Ahead to 2026
postData.summary: The Detroit Lions' 2025 season saw an unprecedented injury crisis in their secondary, earning it the #2 spot in the team's top injury news of the year. With the entire starting unit sidelined by Week 7, the 'Legion of Whom' stepped up. Now, with significant carryover into 2026, the team faces lingering uncertainties despite offseason reinforcements.

sources.title: Detroit Lions Top 10 Injury News 2025: No. 2 The Decimated Secondary
